I'm not exactly sure how this AI is meant to assist, they don't really go into it. I mean it obviously controls drive, but what else? And to what end? It can't make the robot go any faster or hit any harder, so what is it doing? Is it calculating angles of attack? Does it automatically target wheels and the rear of other bots? Where is AI applied? Like most AI, it's a solution looking for a problem to solve. What problem is AI solving?

The production value is definitely improving overall.  One of the main things I like about these vs the older Discovery shows is that a lot less time is spent on the repetitive pre-fight work-up. There were so many better things they could have spent air-time on. What I really want to see from these episodes is more video from the pits. Things like the damage overview of the previous episode at about the 12 minute mark, more views of the guts of the robots being worked on, explanations from the builders of how they work, etc. The engineering that happens on the back end is severely under-represented and needs far more air time.

...but this episode seems to have taken a few steps backwards in certain areas:

- The shifts in brightness (7:51 vs 7:54), contrast (11:06 vs 11:08), color (21:29 vs 21:32) , focus (21:10) and even just baseline image quality (20:40) from camera to camera can  be rather extreme. Many of the in-box views of the fights in particular were very dark and low-contrast. For an environment that's pretty static overall, this shouldn't really be that hard to deal with: the entirety of video stock from each camera should be graded as a whole before even being dumped onto the pile for editing.

- The camera follow was particularly twitchy and hard to watch for the first fight, contrasting the rather slow pace of the first half of the fight itself...

- Several major audio issues: severely limited box audio in several fights, distant and echo-y audio for commentators at several points, etc.

I also want to take the opportunity to make a more general comment on how most shows are cut these days... I'd much rather see the fight than the drivers or especially the commentators, but the solution has been around since literally 1976: picture-in-picture.  If the drivers or commentators have a reaction that's particularly worth seeing, it can be overlaid on top of the fight video instead of completely replacing it and disrupting continuity.  I'm even watching PiP right now in Season 4 when the prerecorded comments from the teams are overlaid on top of the teams prepping in the driver's box.  If it can be used during eminently non-critical scenes like that, there's no reason it can't be used when it actually matters during the fights.  This is what makes drives me completely crazy when seeing things like AGT - I want to see the *act*, not a dumb expression from some random audience member....

It's fantastic that BB is back and available to everyone. Thank you, Bright Data. I do wish though, that the format was the same as it used to be. 20 minutes is way too short, there are not enough interviews, not enough technical information, no real warm-up to the fights, the Tap Out button is weird but I can kind of understand it as it saves a lot of damage to expensive machines. The editing is still rushed, the sound it terrible, much lower volume than anything else on YT, and the original BB music is no longer used, which used to be epic. 

The sounds from the arena are muted down; Kenny and Chris are also muted down. Whilst I would sooner have this than not at all, if you go back and watch the previous WC series, it's chalk and cheese; the latter looks professionally made, whilst the current stuff looks like it was put together from phone footage. Please try to improve it if you can, but if not, I and I am sure many will be thankful that it's back.

@8:47 "they said some really smart people stuff" .. i thought it was COMMON SENSE. what is there to understand? is his IQ too tiny to understand MAGNETS?

it's common sense:
1) arena floor is steel or some other stronger type of metal.
2) magnets are strongly attracted to some metals, like steel and iron.
3) when you put magnet under your bot, it pulls to the ground, making it equivelant to more than 2x the weight of the bot

i don't understand how someone can't understand this.. it's literally common sense.

Episode thoughts:

Multibots typically don't work. But also, multibots aren't typically built by a team with Aren Hill. An amazing first showing from The Twins.

Bloodsport needs to go back to their first design with the three prong blade. Every version since has been subpar to say the least. Classic Minotaur though,

Witch Doctor put on a typical Witch Doctor performance, and Orbitron either doesn't have strong enough motors in their weapons to bounce, or need a self righter. Although since I'm heavily Anti-AI, I may be being too critical.
Humans developed for 40000 years since the caveman era. And the best fighting strat is still "you take the front I take the back" like hunting mammoth😂
